In-House IT
Your own IT Crowd

What   i s  I n-House IT?


Refers to the IT staff, processes, and computers and located inside your business


The Good: They  ’re always there! They know the business. They know the IT systems and maybe even
built them.


The Bad: Employees are costly. You’ve got to keep their skills up-to-date. You’ve got to keep the
technology up-to-date. You’ve got to keep them motivated. They can become disconnected from the
re al   b us i ne s s .   They   are   not   core   b us i ne s s   ( t hi s   has   chang e d   t houg h! )


The Decisions: How can we ensure In-house IT aligns with the business? Should we centralise                           or
distribute In-House IT across the business? How much In-House IT do we need? How much In-House
IT can we afford? What can be outsourced?

                                                                                                                                                                                 This can get
                                                                                                                                                                                 pretty
                                                                                                                                                                                 contested!
























https://www.superfast-
it.com/hubfs/Pros%20and%20cons
%20of%20managed%20IT%20servic
e%20providers.svg

Big Tech
Big Benefits or Big Brother?

What   i s  ‘ Bi g  Tech’ ?


Also known as the Te     c     h          G     i     a     n    t     s           o     r           t     h    e           B     i     g           4. Term became popular in the mid 2010s. These players
have huge influence on the IT sector, and now, almost all other sectors.


The Good: Best IT talent; high quality products and services at low cost or free; innovative; stable
(as far as you can be in IT)


The Bad: Potential for oligarchy or outright monopoly; fundamentally financially profit driven;
inordinate influence over political and economic system (in general, this is understood as bad)


The Decisions:
If I’m part of big tech, how do a protect my turf? If I’m not big tech, when should I use, when
should I not?  What are the risks? Are there even alternatives?

See more:
https://www.statista.com/chart/12991/how-old-
are-your-favorite-tech-companies/

      Does Bi g Tech deli ver soci al good?


      The platforms are now critical components for government and business crisis management


      Could be internal organisational issues like mass redundancies or accident alerts


      Can be an external crisis like covid19 response


      Social networks can blow up a situation in a matter of minutes (and this can be good or bad …
      fake news)

See more: https://au.pcmag.com/security/60591/what-does-big-tech-know-about-you-basically-everyt hi ng

Does Bi g Tech bri ng busi ness ri sks?


Do we have enough control over our profile?


How much does sit cost to manage?


What about negative reviews?


If we limit interaction do we lose traction and transparency ?


Do the platforms have too much control?


Do we rely on the platform too much?


What if we are ‘cancelled’?

Slide
25

IT Consulting
Solutions or Snake Oil?

What   i s  I T  Consul t i ng?


These are firms or individual players who specialize in advising on, and operationalizing, IT
solutions for a specific business


The Good: Interesting and varied work; are experts in implementation; not tied down by the
history in the organization or regular organizational processes; are up-to-date in skills and
knowledge (which is their core business)


The Bad: Might focus too much on the ‘project ’ and not the long-term sustainability of the client;
Can be superficial with solutions; Client retains no internal memory and may grow no In-House IT
capacity


The Decisions:
Who are our core clients? What is our core expertise? How do we keep up-to-date?

https://www.statista.com/chart/16882/trending-topics-consulting/

Tech Start Ups
It all began here…

What   ar e  ‘ I T  St ar t   Ups?


A company in its first stages of operation. Exciting places to be. New boards, new products, new
clients. Usually very small, and  resource and time poor


The Good: Agile and flexible; highly motivated staff; working at the cutting edge of new
technologies and new solutions


The Bad: Most fail (+90%); highly stressful; constant search for capital; easily knocked out of the
market by bigger players if products/services are not protected or easily replicated


The Decisions:
Do we have a unique IT product/service? How unique? How sustainable? How can we scale?
Where can we scale? Can we raise capital? How ? Who?

See more: https://get2growth.com/how-many-startups/
